Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

BRM 4, 06 [Lunar eclipse rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x ⸢U₃ RA⸣ [...]

(o 1') [...] ... [...]

o 2'2'

[...] ne₂-peš ša₂ ŠU-MIN lu₂GALA ša₂ DAR? x [...]

(o 2') [...] the ritual procedure in the domain of the kalû-priest of ... [...]

o 3'3'

[x x] ⸢it?-ta?⸣-ṣi rim-tu₂ TA bi-it dNIN.GAL sin-niš-tu₄ ša x [...]

(o 3') [...] 'She has gone out, the wild cow from the temple of Ningal, the woman of ... [...],

o 4'4'

[sin-niš]-tu₄ um-mu dNIN.GAL ina a-lu it-ta-ṣi

(o 4') 'the woman, the mother, Ningal, has gone out from the city.

o 5'5'

[lu₂?EN?]-ka ša₂ i-dul-lu-ma la i-ṣa-al-lal ana-ku

(o 5') 'I (am) your lord who roams around and does not sleep.

o 6'6'

[x x] ⸢ma⸣-na-aḫ-ti-šu₂ a-kal la i-kul ana-ku ša₂ ina ma-na-aḫ-ti-šu₂ me-e la iš-tu-u₂ ana-ku

(o 6') 'I (am) [...] did not eat bread [...] his weariness. I (am) one who did not drink water in his weariness.

o 7'7'

[x E₂]-⸢ia⸣ ba-aṣ it-taš-pak i-pi-ir it-ta-<at>-bak kab-tu ša₂ di-im-ma-at da-ma-ma ul i-kal-lu

(o 7') 'Sand has been heaped up [...] my house. Dust has been piled up. O dignitary, the one of wailing does not restrain wailing.

o 8'8'

[x ṣer]-ḫi IRI-šu₂ ru-bu-u₂ ina qaq-qar na-pal-si-iḫ me-ḫu-u₂ it-ba-am i-pi-ir pa-ni ik-tu-mu

(o 8') 'The prince squats on the ground [...] lamentation for his city. The tempest arose. It covered dust over the face.

o 9'9'

[u₄]-⸢mu⸣ a-mat d60 u₄-mu a-mat dEN.LIL₂ u₄-mu ug-ga-at lib₃-bi ša₂ d60 ra-bi-i

(o 9') 'The storm (is) the word of Anu. The storm (is) the word of Ellil. The storm (is) the rage in the heart of great Anu.

o 10'10'

⸢dil⸣-mun-nu-u₂ ša₂ ina ṣu-ṣe-e e-lep-šu₂ iṭ-bu-u₂ ana-ku

(o 10') 'I (am) a Dilmunite whose ship sank in the reed-thickets.

o 11'11'

⸢ša₂⸣ lib₃-bi i-šu-u₂ u e-mu-qu la i-šu-u₂ ana-ku : u₂-a lib₃-bi u₂-a ka-bat-ta

(o 11') 'I (am) one who has a heart but does not have strength : woe, my heart, woe, (my) emotions.

o 12'12'

ma-kur-ru ša₂ nin-da-bu-u₂ iz-bil ta-ne₂-eḫ iz-za-bil

(o 12') 'The processional boat which delivered food offerings has delivered distress (instead).

o 13'13'

ša₂ nin-da-be₂-e nin-da-be₂-e ul u₂-še-rib : ša₂ ni-qa-a ni-qa-a ul u₂-še-rib

(o 13') 'The one (in charge) of the food offerings did not bring in the food offerings : the one (in charge) of the libations did not bring in the libations.'

o 14'14'

an-na-a ša₂ a-na d30 ina AN.GE₆ iz-za-mi-ir : ina KA₂ E₂ DINGIR-MEŠ u SILA.DAGAL.LA ga-rak-ku ŠUBdi

(o 14') This (is) what was sung to Sin at the eclipse. : You lay out an altar at the gate of the temple of the gods and (in) the square.

o 15'15'

gišEREN gišŠUR.MIN₃ šimGIR₂ GI DU₁₀.GA gišŠINIG KUR.RA u lu-te-e gišMA.NU ina muḫ-ḫi ga-rak-ku te-ṣe-en

(o 15') You pile cedar, cypress, myrtle, sweet reed, tamarisk of the mountain and twigs of ēru-tree on the altar.

o 16'16'

ki-ma AN.GE₆ u₂-ša₂-ru-u₂ lu₂KU₄.E₂ GI.IZI.LA₂ i-qa-da-ma it-ti ga!(TA-)rak-ku u₂-ša₂-aṣ-ba-at

(o 16') As the eclipse begins, a temple-enterer lights a torch and kindles (fire) at the altar.

o 17'17'

lu₂GALA TUŠab-ma ne₂-peš ša₂ ŠU-MIN lu₂GALA a-di AN.GE₆ u₂-nam-mir DU₃uš

(o 17') A kalû-priest sits and performs the ritual procedure in the domain of the kalû-priest until the eclipse has become light.

o 18'18'

a-di AN.GE₆ u₂-nam-mar IZI ina muḫ-ḫi ga-rak-ku la te-bel-<le>

(o 18') Until the eclipse becomes light, the fire on the altar must not be extinguished.

o 19'19'

ki!(DI-)is-pi a-na A.GAR₃ ŠUB-MEŠ ta-kas₃-sip₄ ki-is-pi a-na ID₂-MEŠ ša₂ me-e la ub-bal KI.MIN<(ta-kas₃-sip₄)>

(o 19') You make funerary offerings for the fallow meadows. Ditto funerary offerings for the watercourses which do not carry water.

o 20'20'

ki!(DI-)is-pi a-na dA.NUN.NA.KI ta-kas₃-sip₄

(o 20') You make funerary offerings to the Anunnakku.

o 21'21'

a-di AN.GE₆ u₂-nam-mar UN-MEŠ KUR ṣu-bat SAG.DU-šu₂-nu ša₂-aḫ!(GU₄-)ṭu ina lu-bar-ra-šu₂-nu SAG.DU-su-nu kat₂-mu

(o 21') Until the eclipse becomes light, the people of the land, their head garments taken off, their heads covered with (just) their pieces of cloth, cry out 'May confinement, murder, rebellion and the eclipse not come close to Uruk, Reš, Irigal, Baramah, Eana and the temples of the gods of Tirana'.

o 22'22'

me-ser₂ ner-ti bar-tu₄ u AN.GE₆ a-a iṭ-ḫu-u₂ a-na UNUGki e₂re-eš IRI₁₂.GAL BARA₂.MAḪ E₂.AN.NA

o 23'23'

u₃ E₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ dTIR.AN.NAki i-ša₂-as-su-u₂ a-na ṣi-ri-iḫ-ti ri-gim-šu₂-nu i-nam-du-u₂

(o 23') They raise their voice on account of lamentation.

o 24'24'

7 lu₂EREN₂ni UN-MEŠ ma-a-tu₂ imṭe!(KI-)ru-ut ID₂ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ŠU-MIN-MEŠ-šu₂-nu u GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂-nu pa-aš₂-šu₂

(o 24') 7 soldiers, people of the land, their faces, their hands and their feet smeared with mud from the watercourse -- you hang a sword on their right collarbones -- cry out 'May confinement, murder, rebellion and the eclipse not come close to Uruk, Reš, Irigal, Baramah, Eana and the temples of the gods of Tirana'.

o 25'25'

GIR₂ ina ker-ri 15-šu₂-nu ta-al-lal

o 26'26'

me-ser₂ ner-ti bar-tu₄ u AN.GE₆ a-a iṭ-ḫu-u₂ a-na UNUGki e₂re-eš IRI₁₂.GAL BARA₂.MAḪ E₂.AN.NA

o 27'27'

u₃ E₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ dTIR.AN.NAki i-ša₂-as-su-u₂ a-na ṣi-ri-iḫ-ti ri-gim-šu₂-nu i-nam-du-u₂

(o 27') They raise their voice on account of lamentation.

o 28'28'

a-di AN.GE₆ i-zak-ku-u₂ i-ša₂-as-su-u₂ : ki-ma ša₂ d30 AN.GE₆ u₂-nam-mar

(o 28') Until the eclipse becomes clear they cry out.

(o 28') As soon as Sin lightens the eclipse, you extinguish the fire on the altar with beer.

o 29'29'

IZI ina muḫ-ḫi ga-rak-ku ina KURUN.NAM tu-kab-bat

o 30'30'

ina 2i u₄-mu lu₂ŠITIM ga-rak-ku a-di ṭi-ik-me-en-šu₂ IL₂ši-ma a-na ID₂ ŠUBdi

(o 30') On the next day a builder lifts up the altar together with its ash and throws (it) into a watercourse.

Reverse
r 1r 1

ina 2i u₄-mu la-am dUTU KURḫa KA₂-MEŠ ⸢ša₂ tu⸣-kan-nik₅ BADte giURI₃.GAL ZI₃.SUR.RAa

(r 1) On the next day, before Šamaš rises, you open the gates which you have sealed. You ... the standard, magic circle (and) white and black twine. You throw (them) into a watercourse. You wipe clean with purification ceremonies all the temples of the gods, and (in particular) the Edumununa, the temple of Sin. Sin is clothed. The holy water vessels are set up.

r 22

tug₂U₂.LI.IN BABBARu₂ u GE₆ tu-rab-ba a-na ID₂ ŠUBdi tak-pe-ra-at E₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ DU₃.A.BI

r 33

u₃ E₂.DUMU.NUN.NA E₂ d30 tu-kap-par d30 MU₄.MU₄ dugA.GUB₂.BA uk-tan-nu

r 44

zi d60 den-lil₂-⸢la₂⸣-bi ḫe₂-pad₃-da-eš : niš da-nu₃ u dEN.LIL₂ <lu> tum₃-mu-šu₂-nu-ti

(r 4) (You say, written in Sumerian and then Akkadian:) 'Adjure them by the life of Anu and Ellil.'

r 55

1en lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ 15 E₂ u₃ ša₂-nu-u₂ GUB₃ E₂ EN₂ ud du₇-du₇-a-meš ŠIDnu-u₂

(r 5) One mašmaššu-priest on the right of the temple and a second on the left of the temple recite the incantation 'Butting storms' and the rest of the mašmaššu-priests recite the incantation 'Evil udug-demons'. They adjure them with incantation formulas.

r 66

u₃ šit-ti lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ-MEŠ EN₂ udug ḫul-meš ŠIDnu-u₂ zi-pa-de-e-MEŠ u₂-ta-am-mu-u₂-šu₂-nu-ti

r 77

ina 2i u₄-mu ZI₃.SUR.RAa tak-pe-rat u₃ ga-rak-ku ana ID₂ ŠUBdi

(r 7) On the next day you throw the magic circle, purification wipings and altar into a watercourse.

r 88

ša₂-niš ina u₄-mu AN.GE₆ d30 lu₂SANGA-MEŠ ša₂ E₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ TIR.AN.NAki ina KA₂ E₂ DINGIR-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ga-rak-ku ŠUBdi-u₂

(r 8) Alternatively, on the day of the eclipse of the moon the šangû-priests of the temples of the gods of Tirana lay out an altar at the gate of the temple of their gods.

r 99

nu-ur u₂-ša₂-aṣ-bat me-ser₂ ner-ti bar-tu₄ u AN.GE₆ a-a iṭ-ḫu-u₂ a-na UNUGki e₂re-eš IRI₁₂.GAL

(r 9) They kindle a light. They cry out 'May confinement, murder, rebellion and the eclipse not come close to Uruk, Reš, Irigal, Baramah, Eana and the temples of the gods of Tirana'.

r 1010

BARA₂.MAḪ E₂.AN.NA u₃ E₂-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ TIR.AN.NAki i-ša₂-as-su-u₂

r 1111

a-na ṣi-ri-iḫ-ti ri-gim-šu₂-nu i-nam-du-u₂ a-di AN.GE₆ i-zak-ku-u₂ i-ša₂-as-su-u₂

(r 11) They raise their voice on account of lamentation. Until the eclipse becomes clear they cry out.

r 1212

u₄-mu AN.GE₆ d30 ḫal-ḫal-lat ZABAR MEZE ZABAR LI.LI.IZ₃ ZABAR TA E₂ am-mu-uš-mu IL₂-nim-ma

(r 12) On the day of the eclipse of the moon they carry the bronze halhallatu-drum, the bronze manzû-drum (and) the bronze kettledrum from the storehouse and place (them) at the dais of the balangu-drum. As soon as the eclipse of the moon begins, kalû-priests, clothed in a linen garment, their heads covered with their torn pieces of cloth, raise lamentation, wailing and weeping to Sin during the eclipse. You lay down 3 heaps of flour opposite the kettledrum inside a magic circle.

r 1313

it-ti BARA₂ki BALAG GARan ki-ma ša₂ AN.GE₆ d30 TABu₂ lu₂GALA-MEŠ TUG₂ GADA MU₄.MU₄

r 1414

ina lu-bar-šu₂-nu nu-uk-<ku>-su-tu SAG.DU-su-<<šu₂>>-nu kat₂-mu ṣi-ri-iḫ-tu₂ ni-is-sa-ti u bi-ki-ti

r 1515

a-na d30 ina AN.GE₆ na-šu-u₂ 3 ZI₃.DUB.DUB.BU a-na me₂-eḫ-rat LI!.LI!.IZ₃ ina ZI₃.SUR.RAa ŠUBdi

r 1616

1en ku-uk-ku-bu di-im-ti ša₂ ŠINIG qu-ud-du-uš u₃ me-e ina ZA₃ ZI₃.DUB.DUB.BU 3-šu₂-nu

(r 16) You set up one offering vessel (full) of tears from a purified tamarisk and water on the right side of the 3 heaps of flour inside the magic circle, for the fixed procedure (relating) to the kalû-priests opposite Sin during the eclipse.

r 1717

ina ZI₃.SUR.RAa a-na kun-nu ŠU-MIN a-na lu₂GALA-MEŠ a-na me₂-eḫ-rat d30 ina AN.GE₆ GUBan

r 1818

ki-i šal-šu₂ ḫap-rat ši-kin AN.GE₆ am amaš-na GARan me-er-me-er zi gu₇!(NAG-)e a-na lib₃-bi i-ru-bu

(r 18) If a third visible surface (is) the extent of the eclipse, 'The wild bull in his fold' is performed. 'Rain-storms consuming life' penetrates into the heart.

r 1919

a abzu-ŋu₁₀ mu-un-ḫul-am₃ ER₂.ŠEM₄.MA : ki-i 2⸢ta ŠU-MIN⸣ ḫap-rat ši-kin AN.GE₆ am amaš-na

(r 19) The Emesal cult lament (is) 'Alas, he is the one who has destroyed my Abzu'. :

(r 19) If two-thirds of the visible surface (is) the extent of the eclipse, 'The wild bull in his fold' (and) 'Woe, alas, your heart' are performed, and 'Alas, he is the one who has destroyed my Abzu' (and) 'Rain-storms consuming life' penetrate into the heart.

r 2020

u₈-u₂ a-a šag₄-zu GARan u₃ a abzu-ŋu₁₀ mu-un-⸢ḫul⸣-[am₃ me]-er-me-er zi gu₇!(NAG-)e ana ŠA₃ KU₄bu

r 2121

ki-i gam!(BAD-)mar-tu₄ TUR⸢tu₄ ši-kin AN⸣.GE₆ am ⸢amaš⸣-[na ...] ⸢GAR⸣an

(r 21) If a small totality (is) the extent of the eclipse, 'The wild bull in his fold' (and) [...] are performed.

r 2222

u₈-u₂ ⸢a-a šag₄⸣-[zu ...] ⸢amaš⸣-na

(r 22) 'Woe, alas, your heart' [...]. [...], '[...] in his fold' (and) 'Of heaven [...]' [...].

r 2323

an-na [...]

r 2424

⸢ki-ma⸣ [...]

(r 24) As [...]

(rest of reverse missing)
